content.problemStatement:
Dr.D is bored, so he's making up problems to pass the time. Here is one of them.

You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Find the number of 7-tuples of indices (a,b,c,d,e,f,g)(a, b, c, d, e, f, g)(a,b,c,d,e,f,g) such that:


1≤a<b<c<d<e<f<g≤N1 \le a \lt b \lt c \lt d \lt e \lt f \lt g \le N1≤a<b<c<d<e<f<g≤N
Ag<Ae<Af<Ad<Ab<Ac<AaA_g \lt A_e \lt A_f \lt A_d \lt A_b \lt A_c \lt A_aAg​<Ae​<Af​<Ad​<Ab​<Ac​<Aa​
Since this number could be huge, compute it modulo 987654319987654319987654319.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the number of tuples modulo 987654319987654319987654319.
